authFields.description: This filter retrieves CDRs with an end time equal to or later than the specified value. It helps filter records based on the end of a timeframe, ensuring that only records ending after a certain point are selected. The date-time format should adhere to ISO 8601 standards.

The actual energy mix can be specified in an electricity rate, which can then be assigned to the charge point. In that case the energy mix will be provided for roaming sessions.
This information can be gathered from the utility provider or third-party platforms that provide this information to the public.

authFields.label: Min Charge Point Current
authFields.description: Defines what is the lowest current (A) allowed per charge point. New sessions cannot start if any of the existing ones has to be lowered to less than the set minimum rate. This is to ensure that a connected car vehicle actually charge because the vehicles have a minimum charging rate below which they would not accept the charge. If left empty, there is no minimum.

authFields.description: Makes the base fee apply per each home charger owned by the subscriber.

authFields.description: Indicates if charging sessions at personal, commercial+personal charge points are included in the post-paid subscription and would be billed at the end of the billing cycle or when a threshold is met, if there is such.

authFields.description: If this threshold is reached, the amount due for the subscription would be billed immediately instead of waiting for the end of the billing period.

authFields.label: Allowance - Home Chargers Only
authFields.description: When true the allowance can only be used on the home charge points owned by the user. In that case all other allowance restrictions have no effect.

authFields.label: Allowance - Included Countries
authFields.description: The allowance can only be used on charge points in countries in this list. When empty the allowance is valid for charge points in all countries, except the ones explicitly listed in the excludedCountries.

authFields.label: Allowance - Excluded Countries
authFields.description: The allowance cannot be used on charge points in the countries in this list.

authFields.label: Allowance - Exclude Roaming
authFields.description: When true the allowance cannot be used on roaming charge points.

authFields.label: Allowance - Included Partner Charge Points
authFields.description: An array of Partner IDs. The allowance can only be used on charge points of Partners in this list. When empty the allowance is valid for charge points of any Partner, except the ones explicitly listed in the excludedPartnerChargePoints.

authFields.label: Allowance - Excluded Partner Charge Points
authFields.description: An array of Partner IDs. The allowance cannot be used on charge points of Partners in this list.

authFields.label: Allowance - Combined
authFields.description: The amount of kWh included - combined for AC and DC, should be provided if the type is combined.

authFields.label: Allowance - Ac
authFields.description: The amount of kWh included for AC, should be provided if the type is separate.

authFields.label: Allowance - Dc
authFields.description: The amount of kWh included for DC, should be provided if the type is separate.

authFields.label: Visibility Restrictions - Included Partner Users
authFields.description: An array of Partner IDs. The Subscription plan is available only to users invited (and accepted) or created by one of the Partners in this list. When empty the plan is available to all users, except the ones of Partners listed in excludedPartnerUsers.

authFields.label: Visibility Restrictions - Excluded Partner Users
authFields.description: An array of Partner IDs. The Subscription plan is not available to users of the Partners in this list.

authFields.label: Visibility Restrictions - Included User Groups
authFields.description: An array of User Group IDs. The Subscription plan is available only to users with a User Group that is in this list. When empty the plan is available to all users, except the ones with a User Group listed in excludedUserGroups.

authFields.label: Visibility Restrictions - Excluded User Groups
authFields.description: An array of User Group IDs. The Subscription plan is not available to users with a User Group that is in this list.

authFields.label: Fee Per Each Personal Charge Point

Create the price levels according to the duration of the session from the time the charging started.
The first level must have zero value for **firstUnit**.
The last level must have an infinity value for **lastUnit**.

The first level must have zero value for **firstUnit**.

authFields.description: Controls randomness: Lowering results in less random completions. As the temperature approaches zero, the model will become deterministic and repetitive. Between 0 and 2. We generally recommend altering this or top_p but not both.

authFields.label: Top P
authFields.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered. Values <=1. We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

title: Chargebee

authFields.description: Required when the subscription has a contract term. Determines when to cancel the subscription and contract term.

authFields.description: Quantity for the termination fee. Applicable only for quantity-based item prices.

authFields.description: The Chargebee item price ID to subscribe the customer to.

authFields.description: Number of billing cycles before the subscription ends. Defaults to the item price setting.

authFields.description: UTC timestamp in seconds when the trial period ends. Set to 0 for no trial.

authFields.description: Controls randomness: Lowering results in less random completions. As the temperature approaches zero, the model will become deterministic and repetitive. Between 0 and 2. We generally recommend altering this or top_p but not both.

authFields.label: Top P
authFields.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered. Values <=1. We generally recommend altering this or temperature but not both.

authFields.label: Model
authFields.description: The model which will generate the completion.

authFields.label: Temperature
authFields.description: Controls randomness: Lowering results in less random completions. As the temperature approaches zero, the model will become deterministic and repetitive.

authFields.label: Maximum Tokens
authFields.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate. The total length of input tokens and generated tokens is limited by the model

authFields.label: Top P
authFields.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.

authFields.label: Frequency penalty
authFields.description: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model

authFields.label: Presence penalty
authFields.description: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the model

authFields.label: Memory Key
authFields.description: A memory key that will keep the chat history shared across runs and flows. Keep it empty to leave Groq without memory of previous messages.

authFields.label: Roles

authFields.description: The model which will generate the completion. Some models are suitable for natural language tasks, others specialize in code.

authFields.label: Temperature
authFields.description: Controls randomness: Lowering results in less random completions. As the temperature approaches zero, the model will become deterministic and repetitive.

authFields.label: Maximum Tokens
authFields.description: The maximum number of tokens to generate. Requests can use up to 2,048 or 4,096 tokens shared between prompt and completion, don

authFields.label: Top P
authFields.description: An alternative to sampling with temperature, called nucleus sampling, where the model considers the results of the tokens with top_p probability mass. So 0.1 means only the tokens comprising the top 10% probability mass are considered.

authFields.label: Frequency penalty
authFields.description: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on their existing frequency in the text so far, decreasing the model

authFields.label: Presence penalty
authFields.description: Number between -2.0 and 2.0. Positive values penalize new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far, increasing the mode

title: Pdfcrowd
description: Convert web pages and HTML to PDF with advanced formatting options

authFields.label: HTML Content
authFields.description: The HTML content to convert to PDF

authFields.label: Output Filename
authFields.description: The filename for the generated PDF

authFields.label: Page Size
authFields.description: Set the output page size using standard formats (A4, Letter, A3, etc.). Use A4 for international documents, Letter for US-based content, or larger sizes like A3 for posters and presentations.

authFields.label: Page Width
authFields.description: Set custom page dimensions when standard sizes don\

authFields.label: Page Height
authFields.description: Set custom page height for specific formats like receipts, banners, or legal documents. Set to

authFields.label: Orientation
authFields.description: Set the output page orientation to portrait or landscape. Use landscape for wide content like spreadsheets, charts, or dashboards. Use portrait for standard documents and text-heavy content.

authFields.label: Margin Top
authFields.description: Control white space at the top of the page. Increase for header space, formal documents, or annotation room (e.g., 1in or more). Decrease to maximize content area or fit more content (e.g., 5mm to 10mm). Default 0.4in balances readability with space efficiency. Set to 0 for full-bleed designs. Affects where content starts and where headers appear.

authFields.label: Margin Right
authFields.description: Control white space on the right edge of the page. Increase for binding/hole-punch clearance or note-taking space (e.g., 1in or more). Decrease to fit wider content (e.g., 5mm to 10mm). Default 0.4in balances readability with space efficiency. Set to 0 for full-bleed designs. Affects where content wraps and text line length.

authFields.label: Margin Bottom
authFields.description: Control white space at the bottom of the page. Increase for footer space, page numbers, or formal documents (e.g., 1in or more). Decrease to fit more content per page (e.g., 5mm to 10mm). Default 0.4in balances readability with space efficiency. Set to 0 for full-bleed designs. Affects where content ends and where footers appear.

authFields.label: Margin Left
authFields.description: Control white space on the left edge of the page. Increase for binding/hole-punch clearance or note-taking space (e.g., 1in or more). Decrease to fit wider content (e.g., 5mm to 10mm). Default 0.4in balances readability with space efficiency. Set to 0 for full-bleed designs. Affects where content starts horizontally and text line length.

authFields.label: No Margins
authFields.description: Disable all page margins to use the entire page area. Use this for full-bleed designs where content should extend to the page edges, such as posters, certificates, or branded materials. Combine with custom CSS to ensure your content fills the page properly.

authFields.label: Print Page Range
authFields.description: Set the page range to print when you only need specific pages from the conversion. Use this to extract individual pages (e.g.,

authFields.label: Content Viewport Width
authFields.description: Set the viewport width for formatting the HTML content when generating a PDF. Use this to control how responsive designs render - prevent mobile styles from applying when you want desktop layout, or ensure content appears at the right width. Specify a viewport width to control content rendering, ensuring it mimics the appearance on various devices or matches specific design requirements.

authFields.label: Content Viewport Height
authFields.description: Set the viewport height for formatting the HTML content when generating a PDF. Specify a viewport height to enforce loading of lazy-loaded images and affect vertical positioning of absolutely positioned elements within the content.

authFields.label: Content Fit Mode
authFields.description: Specify the mode for fitting the HTML content to the print area by upscaling or downscaling it. Use this to prevent content from being cut off at page edges or to enable smart scaling of oversized content.

authFields.label: Remove Blank Pages
authFields.description: Specify which blank pages to exclude from the output document to create cleaner PDFs. Use

authFields.label: Header URL
authFields.description: Load an HTML code from the specified URL and use it as the page header.

authFields.label: Header HTML
authFields.description: Set the HTML header content with custom styling and dynamic page numbers. Use this to add page numbers, document titles, author names, dates, or company branding to the top of every page. Supports full HTML/CSS for complete design control. Use special CSS classes like pdfcrowd-page-number and pdfcrowd-page-count for dynamic content. Ideal for reports, invoices, and professional documents.

authFields.label: Header Height
authFields.description: Set the header height to allocate space for header content and prevent overlap with main content. Increase this if your header text is getting cut off or overlapping with page content. Must be large enough to accommodate your header HTML including any multi-line text or images.

authFields.label: Footer URL
authFields.description: Load an HTML code from the specified URL and use it as the page footer.

authFields.label: Footer HTML
authFields.description: Set the HTML footer content with custom styling and dynamic page numbers. Use this to add page numbers, copyright notices, document dates, or company information to the bottom of every page. Supports full HTML/CSS for complete design control. Use special CSS classes like pdfcrowd-page-number and pdfcrowd-page-count for dynamic content. Ideal for contracts, reports, and official documents.

authFields.label: Footer Height
authFields.description: Set the footer height to allocate space for footer content and prevent overlap with main content. Increase this if your footer text is getting cut off or overlapping with page content. Must be large enough to accommodate your footer HTML including any multi-line text or images.

authFields.label: No Header Footer Horizontal Margins
authFields.description: Disable horizontal page margins for header and footer. The header/footer contents width will be equal to the physical page width.

authFields.label: Exclude Header On Pages
authFields.description: The page header content is not printed on the specified pages. To remove the entire header area, use the conversion config.

authFields.label: Exclude Footer On Pages
authFields.description: The page footer content is not printed on the specified pages. To remove the entire footer area, use the conversion config.

authFields.label: Header Footer Scale Factor
authFields.description: Set the scaling factor (zoom) for the header and footer.

authFields.label: Page Numbering Offset
authFields.description: Set the numbering offset for page numbers in header/footer HTML to continue page numbering from a previous document. Use this when generating document sections separately - for example, if you have already generated pages 1-10, set offset to 10. The next section will then start numbering at page 11. Essential for multi-part reports or book chapters.

authFields.label: Page Watermark URL
authFields.description: Load a file from the specified URL and apply the file as a watermark to each page of the output PDF. A watermark can be either a PDF or an image. If a multi-page file (PDF or TIFF) is used, the first page is used as the watermark.

authFields.label: Multipage Watermark URL
authFields.description: Load a file from the specified URL and apply each page of the file as a watermark to the corresponding page of the output PDF. A watermark can be either a PDF or an image.

authFields.label: Page Background URL
authFields.description: Load a file from the specified URL and apply the file as a background to each page of the output PDF. A background can be either a PDF or an image. If a multi-page file (PDF or TIFF) is used, the first page is used as the background.

authFields.label: Multipage Background URL
authFields.description: Load a file from the specified URL and apply each page of the file as a background to the corresponding page of the output PDF. A background can be either a PDF or an image.

authFields.label: Page Background Color
authFields.description: Set a solid background color for all pages, filling the entire page area including margins. Useful for creating branded PDFs with corporate colors, highlighting draft documents, or improving readability with off-white backgrounds. Supports transparency with RGBA format - use semi-transparent colors for subtle effects without obscuring content. Format as RGB (FF0000) or RGBA (FF000080) hexadecimal.

authFields.label: Use Print Media
authFields.description: Use the print version of the page if available via @media print CSS rules. Enable this when converting websites that have print-optimized styles. Many sites hide navigation, ads, and sidebars in print mode. Produces cleaner PDFs by using the design the website creator intended for printing.

authFields.label: No Background
authFields.description: Do not print the background graphics to create printer-friendly PDFs. Use this when documents will be physically printed to save ink costs and improve readability. Removes background colors, images, and patterns while preserving text and foreground content. Particularly useful for documents with dark backgrounds or decorative elements.

authFields.label: Disable JavaScript
authFields.description: Do not execute JavaScript during conversion. Use this to improve conversion speed when JavaScript is not needed, prevent dynamic content changes, or avoid security risks from untrusted scripts. Note that disabling JavaScript means lazy-loaded images and AJAX content will not load.

authFields.label: Disable Image Loading
authFields.description: Do not load images during conversion to create text-only PDFs. Use this to significantly speed up conversion, reduce file size, or create accessible text-focused documents. Ideal for converting documentation where images are not needed, reducing bandwidth usage, or creating lightweight PDFs for email distribution.

authFields.label: Disable Remote Fonts
authFields.description: Disable loading fonts from remote sources. Use this to speed up conversion by avoiding font download delays, ensure consistent rendering with system fonts, or work around font loading failures. Note that text will fall back to system fonts, which may change the document\

authFields.label: Load Iframes
authFields.description: Specifies how iframes are handled during conversion. Use

authFields.label: Block Ads
authFields.description: Automatically block common advertising networks and tracking scripts during conversion, producing cleaner PDFs with faster conversion times. Filters out third-party ad content, analytics beacons, and ad network resources. Ideal for converting news sites, blogs, or any ad-heavy content where ads distract from the main message. May occasionally block legitimate third-party content - disable if critical third-party resources are missing.

authFields.label: Default Encoding
authFields.description: Specify the character encoding when the HTML lacks proper charset declaration or has incorrect encoding. Prevents garbled text for non-English content, especially legacy pages without UTF-8 encoding. Set to

authFields.label: Locale
authFields.description: Set the locale for the conversion to control regional formatting of dates, times, and numbers. Use this when converting content for specific regions - for example, set to

authFields.label: HTTP Auth User Name
authFields.description: Set the HTTP authentication user name. Required to access protected web pages or staging environments.

authFields.label: HTTP Auth Password
authFields.description: Set the HTTP authentication password. Required to access protected web pages or staging environments.

authFields.label: Cookies
authFields.description: Set HTTP cookies to be included in all requests made by the converter to access authenticated or session-based content. Use this when converting pages that require login, maintain user sessions, or personalize content based on cookies. Essential for converting member-only areas, dashboards, or any content behind cookie-based authentication. Format as semicolon-separated name=value pairs.

authFields.label: Verify SSL Certificates
authFields.description: Enforce SSL certificate validation for secure connections, preventing conversions from sites with invalid certificates. Enable when converting from production sites with valid certificates to ensure security. When disabled, allows conversion from any HTTPS site regardless of certificate validity - including development servers with self-signed certificates, internal corporate sites with expired certificates, or local testing environments.

authFields.label: Fail On Main URL Error
authFields.description: Abort the conversion if the HTTP status code of the main URL is greater than or equal to 400 (client/server errors). Use this in automated workflows to catch broken URLs or authentication failures early rather than producing invalid PDFs. Ensures your system does not silently generate error page PDFs when source content is unavailable.

authFields.label: Fail On Any URL Error
authFields.description: Abort the conversion if any sub-request (images, stylesheets, scripts) fails with HTTP 400+ errors. Use this for strict quality control when all assets must load successfully.

authFields.label: CSS Page Rule Mode
authFields.description: Specifies behavior in the presence of CSS @page rules to control which settings take precedence. Use

authFields.label: Custom CSS
authFields.description: Apply custom CSS to the input HTML document to modify the visual appearance and layout of your content dynamically. Use this to override default styles, adjust spacing, change fonts, or fix layout issues without modifying the source HTML. Use !important in your CSS rules to prioritize and override conflicting styles.

authFields.label: Custom JavaScript
authFields.description: Run a custom JavaScript after the document is loaded and ready to print. Use this to modify page content before conversion, remove unwanted elements, or trigger specific page states. The script is intended for post-load DOM manipulation (add/remove elements, update CSS, ...). In addition to the standard browser APIs, the custom JavaScript code can use helper functions from our JavaScript library.

authFields.label: On Load JavaScript
authFields.description: Run a custom JavaScript right after the document is loaded. The script is intended for early DOM manipulation (add/remove elements, update CSS, ...). In addition to the standard browser APIs, the custom JavaScript code can use helper functions from our JavaScript library.

authFields.label: Custom HTTP Header
authFields.description: Set a custom HTTP header to be included in all requests made by the converter. Use this to pass authentication tokens to protected sites, add tracking headers for analytics, or provide API keys for accessing private content. Essential when converting content from APIs or internal systems that require special headers for access control.

authFields.label: JavaScript Delay
authFields.description: Wait the specified number of milliseconds to finish all JavaScript after the document is loaded. Use this to ensure lazy-loaded images, AJAX content, or animations complete before conversion. Your license defines the maximum wait time by

authFields.label: Element To Convert
authFields.description: Convert only the specified element from the main document and its children. Use this to extract specific portions of a page (like article content) while excluding navigation, headers, footers, or sidebars. The element is specified by one or more CSS selectors. If the element is not found, the conversion fails. If multiple elements are found, the first one is used.

authFields.label: Element To Convert Mode
authFields.description: Control how CSS styles are applied when converting only part of a page. The

authFields.label: Wait For Element
authFields.description: Wait for the specified element in a source document. Use this when specific dynamic content must be ready before conversion, avoiding unnecessary delays from a fixed JavaScript delay. The element is specified by one or more CSS selectors. The element is searched for in the main document and all iframes. If the element is not found, the conversion fails. Your license defines the maximum wait time by the

authFields.label: Auto Detect Element To Convert
authFields.description: The main HTML element for conversion is detected automatically. Use this when you want to extract article or main content without knowing the exact CSS selector, automatically excluding navigation and sidebars.

authFields.label: Readability Enhancements
authFields.description: Automatically enhance the input HTML to improve readability by removing clutter and reformatting content. Use this when converting web pages with excessive navigation, ads, or sidebars that distract from the main content. Different versions (v1-v4) use progressively aggressive algorithms - start with

authFields.label: Header Footer Scale Factor
authFields.description: Set the scaling factor (zoom) for the header and footer.

authFields.label: JPEG Quality
authFields.description: Set the quality of embedded JPEG images to balance file size and visual quality. Use 100% for archival documents or when image quality is critical. Use 70-85% for web distribution to significantly reduce file size with minimal visible quality loss. Use lower values (50-60%) only when file size is more important than image clarity. Common artifacts below 60% include blockiness and color banding.

authFields.label: Convert Images To JPEG
authFields.description: Specify which image types will be converted to JPEG to reduce PDF file size. Use

authFields.label: Image DPI
authFields.description: Set the DPI of images in PDF to control resolution and file size. Use 300 DPI for professional printing, 150 DPI for everyday documents, 96 DPI for screen-only viewing, or 72 DPI for web distribution. Lower DPI creates smaller files but reduces image quality. Use 0 to preserve original image resolution. Note that this only downscales - it will not upscale low-resolution images.

authFields.label: Enable PDF Forms
authFields.description: Convert HTML forms to fillable PDF forms that users can complete in PDF readers. Use this to create interactive PDFs from HTML forms. Ideal for creating fillable applications, surveys, or order forms that work offline. Details can be found in the blog post.

authFields.label: Linearize
authFields.description: Create linearized PDF. This is also known as Fast Web View. Use this to optimize PDFs for progressive download, allowing users to start viewing the first page while the rest downloads.

authFields.label: Encrypt
authFields.description: Encrypt the PDF to prevent search engines from indexing the contents and add an extra layer of security. Use this for confidential documents, internal reports, or any content you do not want appearing in search results. Combine with a password to require authentication for viewing, or just use encryption alone to prevent indexing while keeping the PDF publicly readable.

authFields.label: User Password
authFields.description: Protect the PDF with a user password to restrict who can open and view the document. Recipients must enter this password to view the PDF. Use this for confidential documents, sensitive data, or content distribution where you want to control access. Combine with permission flags to restrict what users can do after opening.

authFields.label: Owner Password
authFields.description: Protect the PDF with an owner password for administrative control. This password allows changing permissions, passwords, and document restrictions - like a master key. Use different user and owner passwords to give recipients restricted access while retaining full control. The owner password should be kept confidential and different from the user password.

authFields.label: No Print
authFields.description: Disallow printing of the output PDF to protect sensitive content. Use this for confidential documents, copyrighted materials, or preview versions you want to restrict.

authFields.label: No Modify
authFields.description: Disallow modification of the output PDF to maintain document integrity. Use this for official documents, contracts, or records that should not be altered after creation. Prevents recipients from editing content, adding annotations, or extracting pages.

authFields.label: No Copy
authFields.description: Disallow text and graphics extraction from the output PDF to protect copyrighted content. Use this for ebooks, proprietary documents, or materials where you want to prevent easy copying and redistribution.

authFields.label: Title
authFields.description: Set the title of the PDF that appears in PDF reader title bars and document properties. Use descriptive titles for better organization and searchability in document management systems. This metadata helps users identify documents when multiple PDFs are open and improves accessibility for screen readers.

authFields.label: Subject
authFields.description: Set the subject of the PDF to categorize or summarize the document content. Use this to add searchable metadata for document management systems, improve organization in large PDF libraries, or provide context about the document\

authFields.label: Author
authFields.description: Set the author of the PDF for attribution and document tracking. Use this to identify who created the document, important for official documents, reports, or publications. This metadata appears in PDF properties and helps with document management and version control.

authFields.label: Keywords
authFields.description: Associate keywords with the document to improve searchability in document management systems. Use relevant terms that describe the content, making it easier to find documents later. Separate multiple keywords with commas. Particularly useful for large document repositories or DAM systems.

authFields.label: Extract Meta Tags
authFields.description: Extract meta tags (author, keywords and description) from the input HTML and automatically populate PDF metadata. Use this when converting web pages that already have proper HTML meta tags, saving you from manually setting title, author, and keywords. Ideal for automated conversion workflows where source HTML is well-structured.

authFields.label: Page Layout
authFields.description: Control how pages appear when the PDF opens in viewers that respect these preferences.

authFields.label: Page Mode
authFields.description: Control the initial display mode when the PDF opens.

authFields.label: Initial Zoom Type
authFields.description: Control how the PDF is initially zoomed when opened.

authFields.label: Initial Page
authFields.description: Display the specified page when the document is opened.

authFields.label: Initial Zoom
authFields.description: Specify the initial page zoom in percents when the document is opened.

authFields.label: Hide Toolbar
authFields.description: Hide the viewer\

authFields.label: Hide Menubar
authFields.description: Hide the viewer\

authFields.label: Hide Window UI
authFields.description: Hide user interface elements like scroll bars and navigation controls when the PDF opens. Use this for presentation mode, digital signage, or embedded PDFs where you want the most minimal interface possible. Combines with other UI hiding options for full-screen immersive viewing.

authFields.label: Fit Window
authFields.description: Resize the PDF viewer window to fit the size of the first displayed page when opened. Use this to ensure the PDF opens at an appropriate size rather than filling the entire screen. Particularly useful for small documents, forms, or certificates that look better at actual size.

authFields.label: Center Window
authFields.description: Position the PDF viewer window in the center of the screen when opened. Use this with window resizing to create a professional, centered display for forms, certificates, or small documents. Improves the initial viewing experience by avoiding corner-positioned windows.

authFields.label: Display Title
authFields.description: Display the title of the HTML document in the PDF viewer\

authFields.label: Right To Left
authFields.description: Set the predominant reading order for text to right-to-left. This option has no direct effect on the document\

authFields.label: Data String
authFields.description: Set the input data for template rendering. The data format can be JSON, XML, YAML or CSV.

authFields.label: Data Format
authFields.description: Specify the input data format. Use

authFields.label: Data Ignore Undefined
authFields.description: Ignore undefined variables in the HTML template. The default mode is strict so any undefined variable causes the conversion to fail. You can use {% if variable is defined %} to check if the variable is defined.

authFields.label: Data Auto Escape
authFields.description: Auto escape HTML symbols in the input data before placing them into the output.

authFields.label: Data Trim Blocks
authFields.description: Auto trim whitespace around each template command block.

authFields.label: Data Options
authFields.description: Set the advanced data options as comma separated key=value pairs: csv_delimiter - The CSV data delimiter, the default is ,. xml_remove_root - Remove the root XML element from the input data. data_root - The name of the root element inserted into the input data without a root node (e.g. CSV), the default is data.

authFields.label: Debug Log
authFields.description: Turn on debug logging to troubleshoot conversion issues. Details about the conversion process, including resource loading, rendering steps, and error messages are stored in the debug log. Use this when conversions fail or produce unexpected results.

authFields.label: Tag
authFields.description: Tag the conversion with a custom value for tracking and analytics. Use this to categorize conversions by customer ID, document type, or business unit. The tag appears in conversion statistics. A value longer than 32 characters is cut off.

authFields.label: HTTP Proxy
authFields.description: A proxy server used by the conversion process for accessing the source URLs with HTTP scheme. This can help circumvent regional restrictions or provide limited access to your intranet.

authFields.label: HTTPS Proxy
authFields.description: A proxy server used by the conversion process for accessing the source URLs with HTTPS scheme. This can help circumvent regional restrictions or provide limited access to your intranet.

authFields.label: Layout DPI
authFields.description: Set the internal DPI resolution used for positioning of PDF contents. It can help in situations where there are small inaccuracies in the PDF. It is recommended to use values that are a multiple of 72, such as 288 or 360.

authFields.label: Main Document CSS Annotation
authFields.description: Add special CSS classes to the main document\

authFields.label: Header Footer CSS Annotation
authFields.description: Add special CSS classes to the header/footer\

authFields.label: Conversion Config
authFields.description: Configure conversion via JSON. The configuration defines various page settings for individual PDF pages or ranges of pages. It provides flexibility in designing each page of the PDF, giving control over each page\

authFields.label: Converter Version
authFields.description: Set the converter version. Different versions may produce different output. Choose which one provides the best output for your case.

authFields.label: Page Watermark
authFields.description: Apply the first page of a watermark PDF to every page of the output PDF. Use this to add transparent overlays like

authFields.label: Multipage Watermark
authFields.description: Apply each page of a watermark PDF to the corresponding page of the output PDF. Use this for page-specific watermarks where different pages need different overlays - for example, different approval stamps per department. If the watermark has fewer pages than the output, the last watermark page is repeated for remaining pages.

authFields.label: Page Background
authFields.description: Apply the first page of a background PDF to every page of the output PDF. Use this to add letterheads, branded templates, or decorative backgrounds that appear behind your content. Backgrounds appear beneath content, while watermarks layer on top. Perfect for adding company letterheads to reports or applying branded templates to dynamically generated content.

authFields.label: Multipage Background
authFields.description: Apply each page of a background PDF to the corresponding page of the output PDF. Use this for page-specific backgrounds where each page needs a different template - for example, different letterheads for front and back pages. If the background has fewer pages than the output, the last background page is repeated for remaining pages.

authFields.label: URL
authFields.description: The URL of the web page to convert. Must be publicly accessible.

title: PDFMonkey
description: Run `turbo run build --filter=@activepieces/piece-pdfmonkey` to build the library.

authFields.label: Payload
authFields.description: Data to use for the Document generation.

authFields.label: Meta
authFields.description: Meta-Data to attach to the Document.

title: PeekShot
description: Run `turbo run build --filter=@activepieces/piece-peekshot` to build the library.

authFields.label: Image Format
authFields.description: Output format for the screenshot.

authFields.label: Custom CSS
authFields.description: Custom CSS to apply.

authFields.label: Custom JavaScript
authFields.description: Custom JavaScript to apply.

authFields.label: Full Page?
authFields.description: To capture the entire page.

title: Perplexity AI
description: AI powered search engine

authFields.label: Temperature

authFields.label: Response Format
authFields.description: Choose the format for the API response. JSON is recommended for most use cases.

authFields.label: Include Today Count
authFields.description: Include the number of entries submitted today in the response. Useful for daily analytics and monitoring.

authFields.label: Field ID
authFields.description: The form field to search in (e.g., Field1 for first name, Field218 for email). You can find Field IDs in the form builder or via the Form Fields API.

authFields.label: Search Value
authFields.description: The value to search for in the specified field. For exact matches, use the complete value.

authFields.label: Match Type
authFields.description: How to match the search value with field data. Choose based on your use case.

authFields.label: Maximum Results
authFields.description: Limit the number of results returned (1-50). Lower numbers are faster for deduplication checks.

authFields.label: Sort Order
authFields.description: Order to return results.

authFields.label: Include Metadata
authFields.description: Include additional information like submission date, IP address, and creation details.

authFields.label: Specific Entry ID (Optional)
authFields.description: Enter a specific Entry ID to retrieve just that entry. Leave blank to get multiple entries with other filters.

authFields.label: Filter Field ID (Optional)
authFields.description: The field ID to filter by (e.g., Field1, Field105, EntryId, DateCreated). Leave blank for no filtering.

authFields.label: Filter Operator
authFields.description: How to compare the filter value with the field data.

authFields.label: Filter Value (Optional)
authFields.description: The value to filter by. For dates, use YYYY-MM-DD format or YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS for specific times.

authFields.label: Second Filter Field (Optional)
authFields.description: Add a second filter field for more complex queries.

authFields.label: Second Filter Operator
authFields.description: Operator for the second filter.

authFields.label: Second Filter Value (Optional)
authFields.description: Value for the second filter.

authFields.label: Filter Logic
authFields.description: How to combine multiple filters. Only applies when using multiple filters.

authFields.label: Sort By Field ID (Optional)
authFields.description: Sort results by field ID (e.g., EntryId, DateCreated, Field1). Leave blank for default order.

authFields.label: Sort Direction
authFields.description: Order to sort the results.

authFields.label: Page Start
authFields.description: Starting entry number for pagination (0 = first entry).

authFields.label: Page Size
authFields.description: Number of entries to return (maximum 100 per request).

authFields.label: Include System Fields
authFields.description: Include additional metadata like IP address, payment status, and completion status.

authFields.label: Subdomain
authFields.description: Your Wufoo account subdomain (e.g., for fishbowl.wufoo.com, use

authFields.label: Handshake Key (Optional)
authFields.description: A secret key to validate webhook authenticity and prevent unauthorized requests. Recommended for production workflows.

authFields.label: Webhook Response Format
authFields.description: Choose the format for webhook data. JSON is recommended for most automation workflows.

authFields.label: Polling Interval
authFields.description: How frequently to check for new forms. More frequent checking provides faster detection but uses more API calls.

authFields.label: Form Name Filter (Optional)
authFields.description: Only trigger for forms containing this text in their name. Leave empty to monitor all forms.

authFields.label: Include Inactive Forms
authFields.description: Include forms that are not currently active/published. Useful for monitoring all form creation activity.

title: Zoom
description:
1. Go to [marketplace.zoom.us](https://marketplace.zoom.us/) and log in to your account.
  2. In the upper-right corner, click **Develop** then **Build App**.
  3. Select **General App**.
  4. Copy the Client ID and Client Secret.Add Redirect URL and press continue.
  5. Go to **Scopes** from left side bar and add **meeting:write:meeting**, **meeting:read:meeting**, **meeting:read:list_meetings**, **meeting:update:meeting**, **webinar:read:webinar**, and **meeting:write:registrant** as scopes.
